decisions.reasoning: MSFT is down ~11% from avg entry $425 but the drawdown is macro/sector-driven, not company-specific: Azure grew 39% YoY (guiding 39-40% next quarter, above Street consensus) per Microsoft IR (Apr 29, 2026) and Yahoo Finance/market data (Jun 25, 2026), and analyst consensus remains Strong Buy (56 analysts, zero sells, $561 avg PT per CNBC/Forbes late June 2026). The stock fell with the broader AI-capex rotation (worst monthly tech drop since 2008, per market commentary Jun 25, 2026) triggered by a hot May jobs print (172K vs ~80K consensus, Bloomberg/CNBC Jun 5, 2026) that raised rate-hike odds. No earnings miss or guidance cut has occurred. Continuing to hold rather than averaging down or panic-selling into macro noise; SMA signal is flat (not a fresh sell trigger).

sources.headline: Cloud and AI strength fuels third quarter results
MSFT Wall Street consensus Strong Buy, $561 price target
May jobs report 172K beats consensus, raises rate-hike odds

decisions.invalidation: FY26 Q4 earnings show Azure/AI growth decelerating materially, or the FTC OpenAI-partnership investigation (opened Feb 2026) results in forced divestment/access remedies

decisions.reasoning: NVDA is down ~10% (in correction) after a broad semiconductor selloff on Jun 23, 2026 (Micron -10%, Marvell -8%, Sandisk -11%, per CNBC Jun 23-25 2026) tied to AI-infra cost anxiety and the same rate-hike-driven rotation hitting MSFT, plus a new US Commerce Dept move to close a China export loophole for Rubin/Blackwell chips. Fundamentals remain strong: Q1 FY2026 revenue $44.1B (+69% YoY), data center $39.1B (+73% YoY) per Nvidia IR, and 36 of 37 analysts still rate Buy/Strong Buy. However the bear case is more substantive than for MSFT -- Michael Burry has a public short thesis (~7% of his book in bearish semi bets), customer concentration (4 customers = 61% of revenue) raises circular-financing concerns, and valuation is stretched at 47x earnings/60x FCF. Holding rather than adding into a name with an active short thesis and no fresh bullish catalyst, but not exiting since the drawdown is sector-wide rotation, not an NVDA-specific guidance failure.

sources.headline: Q1 FY2026 revenue $44.1B, data center revenue $39.1B, +73% YoY
Michael Burry discloses bearish semiconductor bets, calls NVDA a cheap way to short the AI data center bubble

decisions.invalidation: Evidence that the China export-loophole closure materially cuts guided China revenue, or a quarter showing data-center revenue growth decelerating below ~40% YoY

decisions.reasoning: QQQ is down only 1.65%, consistent with a tech-concentrated rotation rather than a broad market event -- SPY is up 1.75% over the same period, confirming this is sector/rate-driven, not systemic. SMA signal is flat. No action warranted; continuing to hold as core broad-tech beta alongside single-name AI exposure (MSFT, NVDA).

sources.headline: Nasdaq underperforms S&P on AI-capex rotation and rate-hike odds

decisions.invalidation: Sustained divergence where QQQ drawdown accelerates well beyond SPY on further Fed hawkishness

decisions.reasoning: SPY is modestly positive (+1.75%) and signal is flat. No research finding today changes the broad-market thesis; kept as the diversified anchor against concentrated tech/AI exposure in the rest of the book.

sources.headline: May jobs report 172K beats consensus, raises rate-hike odds to ~70% by year-end

decisions.invalidation: Broad market deterioration beyond a tech-sector rotation, e.g. credit stress or a hard landing signal

summary: First session (empty journal): held all four existing positions (MSFT, NVDA, QQQ, SPY) after research confirmed the MSFT/NVDA drawdowns are macro/rate-driven sector rotation, not company-specific thesis breaks; no new positions added, no trades placed.
open_theses: MSFT: Azure/AI growth intact (39% YoY), watching FY26 Q4 earnings and the FTC OpenAI-partnership probe for thesis-breaking news
NVDA: revenue growth intact (+69-73% YoY) but active short thesis (Burry) and China export-loophole closure are real risks worth monitoring; will not average down without a fresh bullish catalyst
Watching Fed policy path (rate-hike odds ~70% by year-end per Jun 5 jobs report) as the dominant macro driver of further AI/tech rotation risk
